articles.description: Chief football writer Phil McNulty examines the state of the Premier League after a disappointing week in Europe for all six of its clubs.
articles.title: Premier League: Six teams, no wins in Champions League last-16 first legs

articles.description: Jack Draper produces a superb fightback to beat Novak Djokovic and reach the Indian Wells fourth round - a result he believes could be "a real big moment" in his season.
articles.title: Indian Wells: Jack Draper fights back for 'huge' Indian Wells win over Novak Djokovic

articles.description: Champions League reaction live: English clubs lose heavily in their last-16 first-legs and Arsenal draw at Bayer Leverkusen.
articles.title: Champions League LIVE: Reaction as Premier League sides lose heavily in Europe

articles.description: Live text commentary of the Scotland and Ireland team announcements ahead of Saturday's Six Nations showdown at the Aviva Stadium in Dublin.
articles.title: Six Nations 2026: Scotland team announcement for Ireland Triple Crown decider

articles.description: Max Verstappen says he wishes F1 was "more fun" but is hopeful the sport is heading towards changes that will "improve everything".
articles.title: Chinese Grand Prix: Max Verstappen says he wishes new F1 cars were 'more fun' but he is hopeful of change

articles.description: Rory McIlroy says he hopes to be able to defend his Players Championship title at TPC Sawgrass after withdrawing from a tournament through injury last week.
articles.title: Players Championship: Rory McIlroy hopeful on Sawgrass after injury

articles.description: After Tottenham's Champions League collapse on Tuesday, the appointment of Igor Tudor and the tactics employed increasingly seems to have made their situation worse.
articles.title: Tottenham: Can Spurs afford the time Igor Tudor's tactics need?

articles.description: Harry Hardwick counts himself among the lucky ones who chose to pursue the "stupid" career of MMA.
articles.title: UFC: Harry Hardwick on working night shifts and living with parents aged 31 before UFC chance

articles.description: BBC Sport examines whether Everton can win for a fifth time in six away games at Premier League leaders Arsenal.
articles.title: Will Everton's formidable away form upset Arsenal's title challenge?

articles.description: Joe Root, Adil Rashid and Jonny Bairstow are among the players going under the hammer as the eight men's Hundred sides build their squads for the 2026 tournament.
articles.title: The Hundred 2026: Men's auction with Joe Root, Adil Rashid & Jonny Bairstow going under hammer
